title: Dolomiaea forrestii
taxonomy: Dolomiaea forrestii is classified within the family Asteraceae. This classification includes a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as sunflowers or daisies. Dolomiaea forrestii is further classified as a member of the tribe Anthemideae, a group within the Asteraceae family known for their daisy-like flowers and pungent aromas. The genus Dolomiaea is primarily found in the Himalayan region and is characterized by its shrub-like growth habit and small, white flowers. Overall, the classification of Dolomiaea forrestii reflects its unique characteristics and its evolutionary relationships with other flowering plants.

intro: Dolomiaea forrestii is a perennial plant species within the family Asteraceae, native to the Himalayan region. It has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Dolomiaea forrestii include sesquiterpenoids, triterpenoids, and flavonoids, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Dolomiaea forrestii is often prepared as a decoction or an infusion, as well as being used in the production of extracts and tinctures.
